plan: Create `src/ui/refresh-button.ts` — follow the same factory pattern as `share-button.ts`: export `createRefreshButton(container: HTMLElement): HTMLButtonElement`, create a `<button>` with class `refresh-button`, `aria-label` "Generate new site", textContent `↻`, append to container, return the element.

Create `src/styles/refresh-button.css` — identical base styling to `share-button.css` (44x44px, circular, rgba background, pointer-events: auto, hover/focus-visible states). Position at `bottom: 136px; right: 24px;` to stack above the share button (info=24px, share=80px, refresh=136px, each 56px apart).

Refactor `src/main.ts` to extract the seed-resolve-generate-render sequence into a reusable `renderSite(container: HTMLDivElement): string` function so it can be called both on initial load and on refresh clicks. This function should: generate a new seed via `generateSeed()`, call `writeSeedToUrl(seed)`, call `generateSite(seed)`, call `renderHomepage(container, site)`, and return the seed.

Wire the refresh button in `src/main.ts`: import `refresh-button.css`, import `createRefreshButton`, create the button in the overlay, add a click listener that calls the extracted render function with the `generatedSiteContainer`. The initial page load should still use `resolveSeed(readSeedFromUrl())` for the first render (preserving seed-from-URL behavior), but refresh clicks always generate a fresh seed.
Import `generateSeed` from `src/domain/seed.ts` in `main.ts` (it already exports this function) for use in the refresh click handler to create new random seeds.

Write `tests/us-014-refresh-button.test.ts` with tests covering: (1) `refresh-button.ts` file exists, (2) CSS has correct positioning and pointer-events: auto, (3) button has aria-label, (4) button is integrated in main.ts with proper imports, (5) min 44px touch target, (6) clicking generates a new seed and rerenders without browser reload.
Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify all checks pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: verify `createRefreshButton` returns a button element with correct class, aria-label, and text content
Unit test: verify refresh-button.css has `position: absolute`, `bottom: 136px`, `right: 24px`, `pointer-events: auto`, and min 44px dimensions
Integration test: verify main.ts imports refresh-button.css and createRefreshButton
Functional test: simulate click on refresh button, assert that a new seed is generated (different from previous), URL is updated via history API, and `renderHomepage` is called again
Regression test: verify info button (bottom: 24px), share button (bottom: 80px), and refresh button (bottom: 136px) do not overlap
Stress test: click refresh button 10+ times rapidly, verify no duplicate controls or stale DOM elements remain (acceptance criterion #5)
Run full suite: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test`
risks: Rapid clicking could cause race conditions if renderHomepage is slow — mitigate by disabling the button during render or debouncing clicks
Stacking order of 3 buttons (info, share, refresh) needs careful spacing — if future buttons are added, a layout system may be needed

The extracted render function must not re-create overlay UI elements (info/share/refresh buttons) on each call, only regenerate the #generated-site content — otherwise duplicate controls will appear (acceptance criterion #5)
